text: image to be both realistic and to fit these new constraints. It does this by finding a latent vector that produces an image that is similar to the edited image and obeys the edge map of any added lines. It is typical also to add a mask so that only parts of the image close to the edits are changed. EditGAN (Ling et al., 2021) jointly models images and their semantic segmentation masks and allows edits to that mask.

Problem 15.6 Consider a latent variable $\mathbf{z} \sim \mathcal{N}[0, \mathbf{I}]$ with dimension 100. Consider truncating the values of this variable to (i) $\tau = 2.0$, (ii) $\tau = 1.0$, (iii) $\tau = 0.5$, (iv) $\tau = 0.04$ standard deviations. What proportion of the original probability distribution is disregarded in each case?
